data.title: BIT Mining rebrands to SOLAI Limited, shifts focus to AI and blockchain synergy
data.description:
• BIT Mining Limited (NYSE:BTCM) has officially changed its name to SOLAI Limited, following shareholder approval at its October 9, 2025.
  • The company will also change its ticker symbol to "SLAI", effective Monday, October 20, 2025.
  • The rebranding reflects a strategic pivot from cryptocurrency mining to AI-blockchain technology infrastructure.
  • BTCM shares up 4.2% premarket.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gunnison Copper launches C$15M private placement
data.description:
• Gunnison Copper (TSX:GCU:CA) (OTCQB:GCUMF) has announced a non-brokered private placement to raise up to C$15M.
  • The offering includes up to 33.33M units priced at C$0.45 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and half of a warrant; each full warrant is exercisable at C$0.65 for 36 months.
  • Proceeds will fund drilling, metallurgical testing, permitting, and support a pre-feasibility study for the Gunnison Copper Project.
  • Additional funds will be used for U.S. head office expenses, partial debt repayment to Nebari, and general working capital.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tom Lee’s Bitmine Immersion adds $103M more in ethereum
data.description:
• Tom Lee-backed Bitmine Immersion (NYSE:BMNR) purchased over $103 million in ETH, expanding its Ethereum (ETH-USD) treasury holdings to $12.6 billion.
  • The company scooped up another 23,823 ETH (worth about $103.68 million) from BitGo, according to Arkham data shared by Lookonchain on October 10.
  • The latest purchase expanded Bitmine’s total Ethereum holdings to 2.87 million ETH worth $12.6 billion at the current market price.

data.title: Lazard reports prelim average AUM of $257.4B for Q3
data.description:
• Lazard (NYSE:LAZ) reported preliminary assets under management (AUM) of approximately $264.5 billion as of September 30, 2025, compared to $258.37 billion at August 31, 2025. The total reflects $5.4 billion in market appreciation, $0.5 billion in foreign exchange gains, and $0.3 billion in net inflows for the month.
  • The firm’s preliminary average AUM for the third quarter of 2025 was $257.4 billion.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China probes Qualcomm’s acquisition of Autotalks: report
data.description:
China's State Administration for Market Regulation has started an investigation into the months-old Qualcomm's (NASDAQ:QCOM) acquisition of Autotalks, Bloomberg News reported.

Shares of Qualcomm fell about 3% premarket on Friday. 

The regulator said it would look into potential violations of the country’s anti-monopoly law in relation to Qualcomm's acquisition of connected-vehicle technology provider Autotalks, the report added.

Qualcomm did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

China is targeting Qualcomm, whose technology is vital to smartphones and networking, in the run-up to a meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping. The two nations are maneuvering for leverage ahead of the expiration of a U.S.-China trade truce, even at the risk of increasing tensions, the report noted.

Earlier this week, China unveiled new curbs on its exports of rare earths and other critical materials, echoing moves made in response to Trump’s so-called “Liberation Day” tariffs in April.

Besides Qualcomm, Chinese regulators also disclosed a probe into Nvidia's (NVDA) 2020 acquisition of networking gear maker Mellanox. That investigation was unveiled just as Chinese and U.S. officials sparred over trade and other issues during negotiations in Madrid about a month ago.

data.title: MTY Food Group declares CAD 0.33 dividend
data.description:
• MTY Food Group (OTCPK:MTYFF) declares CAD 0.33/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Nov. 14; to shareholders of record Nov. 4; ex-div Nov. 4.
  • See MTYFF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Nomad Foods names Dominic Brisby as CEO-elect
data.description: • Nomad Foods (NYSE:NOMD) announced the appointment of Dominic Brisby as executive president, chief executive officer-elect, and member of the board of directors, effective November 3, 2025.

data.title: Nomad Foods names Dominic Brisby as CEO-elect
data.description:
• Nomad Foods (NYSE:NOMD) on Friday announced the appointment of Dominic Brisby as its new executive president, chief executive officer elect, and member of the Board of Directors, effective November 3, 2025.
  • Brisby will work with the company's current CEO, Stéfan Descheemaeker, for a transition period until Descheemaeker's retirement on January 1, 2026, when Brisby will be appointed CEO.
  • Descheemaeker will continue to serve as a director on the company's board following the CEO transition.
  • Brisby most recently served as president of North America and Europe at Flora Food Group, a plant-based food company.

data.title: Apogee declares $0.26 dividend
data.description:
• Apogee (NASDAQ:APOG) declares $0.26/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.51%
  • Payable Nov. 13; to shareholders of record Oct. 29; ex-div Oct. 29.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.26 for four consecutive quarters.
  • See APOG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Quantum Computing files to sell 37.18M shares of common stock for holders
data.description:
• Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT) has filed a prospectus for the potential resale of up to 37,183,937 shares of its common stock by certain existing stockholders.
  • Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT) itself will not sell any shares or receive any proceeds from these sales.
  • The company said it will cover the costs associated with registering the shares for resale.
